selected_option_label: Port provided UI verbatim + wire to frozen commerce-core engine

criteria: Fits intent (distinct sticker storefront with variant cart + fulfillment)
Reversibility (can we pivot without losing work?)
Compliance (public-safe, validate-contracts pass, not a reskin)

options.label: Port docs/atm-415/ui/marginalia verbatim → wire to commerce-core

scores.fits_intent: HIGH — provided UI implements all required flows: catalog, variant selector, multi-item cart, checkout, fulfillment tracker, cancellation, sell/curator pages
scores.quality_gate: HIGH — UI was authored to the brand; faithful port minimizes design deviation risk
scores.technical_risk: LOW — commerce-core CartLine/Order schema fits variant-cart shape without modification; proven in ATM-417 for same stack
scores.sprint_cost: LOW — no design decisions; only wiring and data layer
scores.reversibility: HIGH — local-only, no deployment; can be iterated at any stage
scores.compliance: PASS — prime directive mandates verbatim port; no copyrighted assets; validate-contracts must pass

options.label: Build custom Marginalia UI from scratch (ignoring provided design)

scores.fits_intent: MEDIUM — could implement correct flows but visual outcome is unpredictable
scores.quality_gate: RISK — without a provided design, visual quality depends on design decisions in this session; QA gate may not pass
scores.sprint_cost: HIGH — requires design decisions, component design, visual QA iteration
scores.reversibility: MEDIUM — code can be changed but design rework is expensive
scores.compliance: FAIL — prime directive explicitly prohibits redesign; 'no improvements, no restyling' is a hard rule

options.verdict: REJECTED — violates prime directive (provided UI must be ported verbatim)

options.label: Reskin video-storefront (OneReel) for sticker domain

scores.fits_intent: FAIL — OneReel has no variant selector, cart, fulfillment tracker, or cancel flow; sticker mechanics are structurally distinct
scores.quality_gate: FAIL — same template would not pass 'not a reskin' gate
scores.reversibility: LOW — hard to un-merge two apps' concerns
scores.compliance: FAIL — spec explicitly requires distinct mechanics (variants, cart, fulfillment, physical economics); 'not a reskin' is a hard acceptance-matrix gate

options.verdict: REJECTED — fails distinctness contract and mechanics requirements

options.label: Simplified single-product checkout (no variants, no cart)

scores.fits_intent: LOW — brand requires 'what you see ships' variant clarity; physical goods require multi-item cart
scores.quality_gate: FAIL — QA would flag missing variant selector and cart as P0 defects
scores.compliance: FAIL — spec and brand doc explicitly require variant selection, cart, fulfillment tracking, and cancellation

options.verdict: REJECTED — incomplete mechanics relative to spec requirements

decision_rationale: OPT-A is the only compliant option: the prime directive mandates verbatim port of the provided UI, and the spec requires variant cart + physical fulfillment mechanics. commerce-core supports the required CartLine schema without changes. ATM-417 (OneReel) demonstrated the same stack works cleanly. The provided UI (docs/atm-415/ui/marginalia) implements all required flows and was authored to the Marginalia brand — porting it faithfully minimizes design risk and satisfies the QA gate.

reversibility.rollback_means: This is a local build only. If QA fails (< 7/10 or any P0), we fix the implementation and rerun QA — no frozen contracts are touched and no external commitments are made. OWNER_GATE is required only if a frozen contract needs to change, which OPT-A does not require.

architecture_decisions.framework: Next.js 15, App Router, TypeScript — same as ATM-417
architecture_decisions.styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4 + CSS variables (Vinyl Pop token system from provided UI)
architecture_decisions.data_layer: packages/commerce-core + localStorage (synthetic, no DB, no auth)
architecture_decisions.font: Baloo 2 via next/font/google (used in provided layout.tsx)
architecture_decisions.ui_source: docs/atm-415/ui/marginalia — ported verbatim, wiring-only edits permitted
architecture_decisions.catalog: lib/packs.ts — 6 synthetic packs with variant pricing, public-safe shape art

hard_gates.evidence: Four options compared (OPT-A through OPT-D) on six criteria each. Three explicitly rejected with reasons.
Reversibility section: yes, local-only build, rollback = fix implementation and rerun QA, no frozen-contract touch required.

option_fairness.evidence: All four options scored on the same six criteria. OPT-B and OPT-C rejected for compliance violations; OPT-D rejected for missing mechanics — not for subjective reasons.

tradeoff_clarity.evidence: OPT-A tradeoffs: sprint cost is lowest but requires faithful port discipline (no visual deviations). Verbal-port rigidity is the constraint, not a hidden cost. OPT-B cost is design rework risk. OPT-C cost is non-distinctness.

fit_to_intent.evidence: Selected option directly satisfies: distinct sticker domain, variant cart, physical fulfillment, curated catalog, impeccable gate, deterministic cohort, frozen engine compliance.

anti_overbuild.evidence: No new engine, no new schema, no new auth system. Plain Next.js + Tailwind + localStorage. The seller/curator pages from the provided UI are included only because they are in the provided UI tree (prime directive) — no additional features added beyond what the UI provides.
